frameworks.name: value-based decision making
frameworks.one_line: Expected reward representations guide choice and learning in cortex.

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Value signals support reward prediction-error updating of choice policy.

frameworks.name: actor-critic dissociation
frameworks.one_line: Separable neural substrates for evaluating versus selecting actions.

core_question: Do orbitofrontal value representations directly drive choice, or instead support learning?
key_assumptions: Value representations can serve both choice and learning roles
A multi-step task can computationally separate learning from choosing
Orbitofrontal cortex is central to value-based cognition
manipulations: Multi-step rodent task dissociating learning from choice
Optogenetic perturbation of OFC value representations
key_findings: OFC value representations do not directly drive choice
OFC value signals carry expected-reward information for a learning process
Perturbing OFC affects updating rather than action selection
interpretations: OFC supplies expected-reward signals to a downstream learner that updates choice mechanisms
Choice-driving and learning-supporting value representations are functionally dissociable
